name: logical_form
description: Natural language to logical form translation. The graph has predicate/proposition nodes; tests whether graph-augmented inference produces more precise logical representations.

cases.question: Translate to predicate logic: 'All dogs are animals.' Use standard notation.
Translate to predicate logic: 'Some birds cannot fly.'
Translate to predicate logic: 'No reptile is a mammal.'
Translate to predicate logic: 'If a mutation breaks DDR, then PARP inhibition causes cell death.'
Is this valid? 'All A are B. All B are C. Therefore all A are C.' Answer yes or no.
Is this valid? 'Some A are B. Some B are C. Therefore some A are C.' Answer yes or no.
Are these contradictory? 'All swans are white' and 'There exists a black swan.' Answer yes or no.
Are these logically equivalent? 'No A is B' and 'No B is A.' Answer yes or no.
Translate: 'Inhibiting PARP1 in BRCA2-deficient cells causes synthetic lethality.' Use Causes/Inhibit predicates.
Translate: 'Most mutations in channel genes are harmful, but some are passengers.' Use quantifiers.
cases.answer: Most(x, ChannelGene(x) ∧ Mutation(x), Harmful(x)) ∧ ∃x(ChannelGene(x) ∧ Mutation(x) ∧ Passenger(x))
